<summary>Agent "kage audit for claude-mem stores" completed</summary>
<result>PR opened: https://github.com/kage-core/Kage/pull/80

## Report

**PR**: #80 — `wedge/claude-mem-audit` → `master` (commit 5190927; 4 files, +566/−1)

**Schema findings from thedotmack/claude-mem** (fresh `--depth 1` clone):
- DB filename: `claude-mem.db` in `DATA_DIR`, which defaults to `~/.claude-mem` but is overridable via `CLAUDE_MEM_DATA_DIR` env or `settings.json` — the spec's assumed default path was correct; I also honor the env var.
- `observations` table (`src/services/sqlite/schema.sql`): `type/title/subtitle/facts/narrative/files_read/files_modified/created_at/created_at_epoch` plus `project`. `files_read`/`files_modified` are `JSON.stringify`'d string arrays, and claude-mem's own `parseFileList` treats non-JSON values as a single raw path — I mirror that leniency. `created_at_epoch` is **milliseconds** (`Date.now()`), `created_at` is ISO text.
- Project keying: not plain cwd basename — `getProjectName` uses the **basename of the git repo root** (stable across worktrees/subdirs, their issue #2663), falling back to cwd basename outside a repo. `claudeMemProjectKey` replicates this.

**Implementation** (all absolute paths under the worktree):
- `mcp/kernel.ts`: `defaultClaudeMemStorePath`, `claudeMemProjectKey`, `parseClaudeMemFileList`, `readClaudeMemObservations` (module-level reader: `node:sqlite` `DatabaseSync` read-only via guarded `require`, fallback `sqlite3 -readonly -json`, friendly error naming what's missing), `buildClaudeMemChangeSignal` (single full-history `git log --name-only` pass — same primitive as `truthReport` — with mtime fallback), pure `classifyClaudeMemObservations` (GONE > DRIFTED > VERIFIED priority, UNCITED for empty citations), `auditClaudeMemStore`, `renderClaudeMemAuditReceipt` (spec format incl. closing line with the real site URL `https://kage-core.github.io/Kage/`).
- `mcp/cli.ts`: `audit-claude-mem` handler (exit 2 + friendly error on unreadable store), `FULL_USAGE` entry only.
- `CHANGELOG.md`: new `## Unreleased` section.

**Tests**: 3 new tests in `mcp/kernel.test.ts` — fixture SQLite db built through the same dual mechanism (skips if neither exists), classified against a temp git repo with `GIT_COMMITTER_DATE`-controlled commits covering all four statuses plus a foreign-project row (excluded), receipt-line assertions; missing-store + project-key-miss warning; pure classifier (ms-vs-s epochs, raw-string file lists, placeholder filtering, status priority). Full `npm test`: **220 pass / 0 fail**. Smoke-tested the CLI end-to-end (receipt, `--json`, missing store).

**Caveats**:
- "Changed since capture" uses newest-commit time (mtime for untracked paths) — a file recommitted with identical content after capture reads as DRIFTED; this is the same conservative bias Kage's staleness checks already use. A repo without git history falls back to mtimes and warns (fresh checkouts reset mtimes).
- Cited paths are model-generated in claude-mem; absolute, relative, and `~/` forms are handled, but paths outside the project can only be existence/mtime-checked.
- Did not run `kage refresh` per instructions; the `node:sqlite` guarded `require` works because the package compiles to CJS (no `"type": "module"`).</result>

<summary>Agent "discovery_tokens + read-time injection" completed</summary>
<result>PR opened: https://github.com/kage-core/Kage/pull/81

## Summary

**PR #81** — branch `adopt/receipts-and-read-context` → `master`, commit `53e4af4`.

### 1. discovery_tokens receipts (`mcp/kernel.ts`, `mcp/cli.ts`, `mcp/index.ts`)
- `CaptureInput`/`LearnInput` gained `discoveryTokens`; `capture()` stores it as `quality.discovery_tokens` + `discovery_tokens_estimated: false`. When omitted, `defaultDiscoveryTokens()` applies the commented conservative map `DEFAULT_DISCOVERY_TOKENS` (bug_fix/gotcha 8000, decision 4000, fallback 2000) flagged estimated. `distillSession()` estimates from session observation material.
- New `replayTokensSaved()`: `sum(discovery_tokens of served packets) − estimateTokens(context block)`, floored at 0. Recall's `value_receipt.tokens_saved` is now `max(read-vs-source, replay)` — never less than prior behavior, never negative; `replay_tokens` is recorded on `recall_served` ledger events, rolled into totals/windows, and `kage gains` prints a "Knowledge replay value" line. Exposed via `kage_learn` schema `discovery_tokens` and CLI `kage learn --discovery-tokens <n>`.

### 2. PreToolUse(Read) injection
- `kageFileContext()` + `kage file-context --project <dir> --path <file> [--json]`: ≤3 verified packets citing the file (filters on `staleMemoryReasons()` being empty — staleness never bypassed), ≤20-line block, empty output when none, records a value event when non-empty.
- `setupAgent()` (claude-code) now writes `~/.claude/kage/hooks/kage-read-context.sh` and adds a `PreToolUse` matcher-`Read` settings entry (timeout 6); script parses `tool_input.file_path`, gates on in-project + `.agent_memory` present, dedups per session+path via `/tmp/kage-read-context/<sha>` state files, emits `hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext` JSON only when non-empty, always exits 0. Mirrored in `plugin/hooks/kage-read-context.sh` + `hooks.json` `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}` entry with keep-in-sync headers. `claudeAmbientHookSummary` now verifies the new script.

### Tests
- 3 new tests (discovery defaults/reported values, receipt floor math both directions, file-context stale-exclusion/cap/empty/outside-project) plus extended setup-agent hook assertions. Full `npm test`: **220 pass, 0 fail**. Also smoke-tested the CLI + hook end-to-end (injection, dedup, gating, gains line).

### Caveats
- `kage_pr_check` is not fully green: it demands `kage refresh` because graph index artifacts (gitignored) don't exist in this fresh worktree — intentionally skipped per your instruction about packet-fingerprint merge conflicts; noted in the PR checklist. The remaining 8 items are soft "linked path changed" reconciliation warnings expected for a branch touching kernel.ts/cli.ts/index.ts.
- The hook relies on the settings-entry timeout (6s) rather than a `timeout` binary (absent on macOS by default).
- `file-context` reuses the `recall_served` ledger kind (no new event type), so injections count as recalls in `kage gains`.</result>

<summary>Agent "Conflict fix + timeline (retry)" completed</summary>
<result>PR created: https://github.com/kage-core/Kage/pull/82

## Report

**PR:** #82 — `fix/packet-conflicts-and-timeline` → `master`

**Behavior summary**

1. **Quiet-refresh on branches** (`mcp/kernel.ts`): `refreshProject` now detects the default branch via `origin/HEAD` (falling back to local `master`/`main` existence). On any non-default branch, `refreshPacketStaleness` runs in quiet mode: staleness is fully computed in memory (findings returned, recall withholding unaffected — recall already recomputes staleness live), but metadata-only rewrites (stale flags / `updated_at` / fingerprint recomputation) are not persisted, so unchanged packets stay byte-identical. Content changes (pruned grounding/citation paths) still write; `--force` (CLI) / `force` (MCP `kage_refresh`) restores full rewrites. `RefreshResult` gains `quiet_refresh` plus a next-action hint.
2. **`kage merge-packet <ours> <base> <theirs>`** (`mcp/cli.ts` + `mergePacketFiles` in kernel): git merge-driver convention — writes result to the ours path, exit 0 merged / exit 1 conflict. Whole-file newest-wins by `updated_at`; sides containing committed conflict markers are recovered via repair's `splitConflictSides`/`resolveConflictedPacket`; garbage on both sides exits 1. Handled before tree-sitter setup so the driver is fast. `kage init` and `kage install` idempotently write `.agent_memory/packets/*.json merge=kage-packet` to `.gitattributes` (replaces a stale driver value in place, never duplicates) and print the `git config merge.kage-packet.driver "npx -y @kage-core/kage-graph-mcp merge-packet %A %O %B"` one-liner.
3. **Resume timeline** (`kageResume`): output ends with `## Recent memory` — one line per packet `[id-prefix(12)] type title (age)` for the newest 15, summary detail only for the newest 3, capped at 800 estimated tokens, only when packets exist; report gains `recent_memory`.
4. **`kage_workflow`** (`mcp/index.ts`): no-op MCP tool; 122-word description teaches the loop (kage_context → work → kage_learn → kage_refresh → kage_pr_check), mentions `<private>` tags and recall receipts; handler returns the identical text.

**Tests:** full `npm test` passes — **230 tests, 0 failures**. New coverage: branch vs default-branch refresh (temp git repos, byte-identity assertion, `--force`), merge driver kernel + CLI exit codes, `.gitattributes` idempotency, timeline ≤15 entries/≤800 tokens, `kage_workflow` listing + description/handler parity. One existing resume test's 15-line assertion was updated (session section still ≤15 lines; timeline follows).

**Caveats**

- Merge driver is whole-file newest-wins (v1, as scoped) — the base side is read but unused; field-level three-way merge is a future refinement. If exactly one side parses, that side wins (mirrors repair's behavior) rather than exiting 1; exit 1 only when both sides are unrecoverable.
- Detached HEAD or undeterminable default branch falls back to full-rewrite refresh (safe default).
- Quiet refresh still allows content rewrites of generated overview/structure packets on branches when repo files genuinely changed — those are content changes, not cosmetic churn.
- `kage init` now touches `.gitattributes` in addition to `.agent_memory/` (explicit requirement; the "init touches only .agent_memory" comment was updated).
- Per instructions, I did not run `kage refresh` against this repo.</result>

<summary>Agent "Personal memory + sync (retry)" completed</summary>
<result>PR created: https://github.com/kage-core/Kage/pull/84

## Summary

**PR #84** — branch `feat/personal-memory-sync` → `master`, full `npm test` green: **236/236 tests, 0 failures** (includes 6 new tests).

### What was built

**Personal store** (`mcp/kernel.ts`, new section at end of file)
- `kageHomeDir()` honors `$KAGE_HOME`, defaulting to `~/.kage`; packets live in `~/.kage/memory/packets`, conflicts in `~/.kage/memory/conflicts`.
- `learnPersonal`/`capturePersonal`: scope `personal`, visibility `private`, same secret-scan and `<private>`-span redaction as repo capture. Cited paths are validated + fingerprinted against the current project (all-missing → rejected); citation-free is allowed only here, stamped `quality.unverifiable: true` / `freshness.verification: "unverifiable_personal"`.
- Strict repo `learn` (CLI/MCP) with zero paths now fails with a pointer to `--personal`. Scoped to `learn`, not `capture` — an existing test depends on path-less `kage_capture` succeeding, and the task spec named learn specifically.
- Recall (`recallWithVectorScores`): appends a "## Personal Memory" section (≤3 packets, every line `[personal]`-tagged, lower-trust framing) after all repo sections; cited packets re-verified per checkout via the existing `recallHardStaleReason` (withheld in clones where evidence is gone). Personal entries go in a new `RecallResult.personal` field, **never** `results`, so pr-check/staleguard/refresh/access-tracking structurally can't see them. Token-budget trimming drops the personal section first (it's last).

**`kage sync`** (kernel functions + CLI commands, run before tree-sitter init since they're pure git)
- `syncSetup`: idempotent init + remote add/set-url + initial commit + `push -u origin HEAD`; a second machine converges on the remote's default branch (`ls-remote --symref`, `branch -M`, rebase) instead of forking a parallel branch.
- `syncPersonal`: commit → fetch → rebase → push via `execFileSync` with explicit cwd; receipt `pushed N, pulled M, resolved K` (packet-file counts from merge-base diffs). Conflict loop: `packets/*.json` resolve newest-`updated_at`-wins (reuses `packetRecency` policy from the merge driver), loser preserved as `conflicts/<name>.<unix-ts>.json` and committed (so it propagates), winner rewritten as clean JSON; empty-after-resolution commits handled via `rebase --skip`; non-packet conflicts abort. No remote/network failure → exit 2 with the git error.
- Falls back to a `kage-sync` git identity when none is configured.

**Tests** (`kernel.test.ts`): the 5 required scenarios, using `$KAGE_HOME` temp dirs and local bare remotes; the conflict test does a real two-machine interleaved rebase conflict and verifies the resolution syncs back. Both test files also pin a hermetic default `KAGE_HOME` so suites never read a developer's real `~/.kage`.

**Docs**: README "Personal memory & sync" subsection after the session-loop paragraph; FULL_USAGE entries; CHANGELOG bullet under "Unreleased".

### Caveats
- `pushed/pulled` counts are packet-file counts across merge-base diffs — an approximation when a conflict resolution discards one side (the receipt may count a file whose content ultimately didn't change).
- Personal memory is CLI-only for now; no `kage_learn_personal` MCP tool was added (not in scope).
- Per instructions I did not run `kage refresh`; Kage MCP tools weren't loaded in this session, so no `kage_pr_check`/`kage_propose_from_diff` was run either — repo memory for this change is carried by the PR itself.</result>

<summary>Agent "Auto-distill quality gate" completed</summary>
<result>Done. PR created: **https://github.com/kage-core/Kage/pull/85**

## Summary

**PR:** #85 — `fix/auto-distill-signal-gate` → `master`

**Scoring approach** (`observationSignalScore` in `/Users/kushaljain/code/Kage/.claude/worktrees/agent-a1eb57dbebe400067/mcp/kernel.ts`, pure and exported):
- **Hard rejects → 0:** combined text under 50 chars; hook/system payload markers (`task-notification`, `tool-use-id`, `toolu_`, `system-reminder`, `hookSpecificOutput`, `stop_hook_active`, ...); Kage self-echo markers (Truth Report, "hallucinated citations never enter storage", `value_receipt`/`stale_withheld`); JSON noise (parses as JSON, ≥3 quoted-key patterns, or >50% JSON punctuation density); flag-token dumps (≥2 `key false/true` pairs covering ≥40% of tokens).
- **Additive positive signal:** causal/imperative vocabulary (+0.25 first hit, +0.05 each additional, cap 0.35), file path citation or `event.path` (+0.2), code identifiers — camelCase/snake_case/backticks/calls (+0.15), command line (+0.15), substantive prose ≥80 chars and ≥10 words (+0.1), clamped to 1.
- **Gate:** exported `AUTO_DISTILL_SIGNAL_THRESHOLD = 0.4` applies only to auto-distill (`distillSession` `auto: true`); gated events counted in `DistillResult.skipped_low_signal`. `observe()` tags below-threshold records `low_signal: true` at ingestion so auto-distill skips without rescoring. Manual learn/capture/distill remain ungated.
- Measured scores: all three reconstructed junk bodies → 0; genuine daemon.ts race-fix learning → 0.7; existing legit fixtures land 0.45–0.6.

**Tests:** full `npm test` passes — **232 tests, 0 failures** (was 230; two new tests added covering the three junk reconstructions, flag dumps, short fragments, ingestion tagging, `skipped_low_signal: 3`, and a genuine learning still drafting). CHANGELOG entry added under `## Unreleased`.

**Caveats:**
- The three original junk packet files were already hand-deleted from the main repo, so test bodies are plausible reconstructions; each was crafted to pass the *legacy* keyword filters, proving the new gate is what blocks them.
- Self-echo markers are phrase-level (`truth report`, `value_receipt`, ...) — a genuine learning *about* Kage's receipt internals that quotes those exact strings would be gated from auto-drafts (manual `kage learn` still works for that case).
- Manual `kage distill` is intentionally ungated (treated as explicit intent, like learn/capture); only the Stop-hook `--auto` path is filtered.
- Per instructions, `kage refresh` was not run. Also note: no Kage MCP tools were loaded in this session, so the harness calls from CLAUDE.md (kage_context/kage_learn/kage_pr_check) could not be made; a restart would be needed to load them.</result>

- Recall (`recallWithVectorScores`): appends a "## Personal Memory" section (≤3 packets, every line `[personal]`-tagged, lower-trust framing) after all repo sections; cited packets re-verified per checkout via the existing `recallHardStaleReason` (withheld in clones where evidence is gone). Personal entries go in a new `RecallResult.personal` field, **never** `results`, so pr-check/staleguard/refresh/access-tracking structurally can't see them. Token-budget trimming drops the personal section first (it's last).

**`kage sync`** (kernel functions + CLI commands, run before tree-sitter init since they're pure git)
- `syncSetup`: idempotent init + remote add/set-url + initial commit + `push -u origin HEAD`; a second machine converges on the remote's default branch (`ls-remote --symref`, `branch -M`, rebase) instead of forking a parallel branch.
- `syncPersonal`: commit → fetch → rebase → push via `execFileSync` with explicit cwd; receipt `pushed N, pulled M, resolved K` (packet-file counts from merge-base diffs). Conflict loop: `packets/*.json` resolve newest-`updated_at`-wins (reuses `packetRecency` policy from the merge driver), loser preserved as `conflicts/<name>.<unix-ts>.json` and committed (so it propagates), winner rewritten as clean JSON; empty-after-resolution commits handled via `rebase --skip`; non-packet conflicts abort. No remote/network failure → exit 2 with the git error.
- Falls back to a `kage-sync` git identity when none is configured.

**Tests** (`kernel.test.ts`): the 5 required scenarios, using `$KAGE_HOME` temp dirs and local bare remotes; the conflict test does a real two-machine interleaved rebase conflict and verifies the resolution syncs back. Both test files also pin a hermetic default `KAGE_HOME` so suites never read a developer's real `~/.kage`.

**Docs**: README "Personal memory & sync" subsection after the session-loop paragraph; FULL_USAGE entries; CHANGELOG bullet under "Unreleased".

### Caveats
- `pushed/pulled` counts are packet-file counts across merge-base diffs — an approximation when a conflict resolution discards one side (the receipt may count a file whose content ultimately didn't change).
- Personal memory is CLI-only for now; no `kage_learn_personal` MCP tool was added (not in scope).
- Per instructions I did not run `kage refresh`; Kage MCP tools weren't loaded in this session, so no `kage_pr_check`/`kage_propose_from_diff` was run either — repo memory for this change is carried by the PR itself.</result>

## Summary

**PR:** #85 — `fix/auto-distill-signal-gate` → `master`

**Scoring approach** (`observationSignalScore` in `/Users/kushaljain/code/Kage/.claude/worktrees/agent-a1eb57dbebe400067/mcp/kernel.ts`, pure and exported):
- **Hard rejects → 0:** combined text under 50 chars; hook/system payload markers (`task-notification`, `tool-use-id`, `toolu_`, `system-reminder`, `hookSpecificOutput`, `stop_hook_active`, ...); Kage self-echo markers (Truth Report, "hallucinated citations never enter storage", `value_receipt`/`stale_withheld`); JSON noise (parses as JSON, ≥3 quoted-key patterns, or >50% JSON punctuation density); flag-token dumps (≥2 `key false/true` pairs covering ≥40% of tokens).
- **Additive positive signal:** causal/imperative vocabulary (+0.25 first hit, +0.05 each additional, cap 0.35), file path citation or `event.path` (+0.2), code identifiers — camelCase/snake_case/backticks/calls (+0.15), command line (+0.15), substantive prose ≥80 chars and ≥10 words (+0.1), clamped to 1.
- **Gate:** exported `AUTO_DISTILL_SIGNAL_THRESHOLD = 0.4` applies only to auto-distill (`distillSession` `auto: true`); gated events counted in `DistillResult.skipped_low_signal`. `observe()` tags below-threshold records `low_signal: true` at ingestion so auto-distill skips without rescoring. Manual learn/capture/distill remain ungated.
- Measured scores: all three reconstructed junk bodies → 0; genuine daemon.ts race-fix learning → 0.7; existing legit fixtures land 0.45–0.6.

**Tests:** full `npm test` passes — **232 tests, 0 failures** (was 230; two new tests added covering the three junk reconstructions, flag dumps, short fragments, ingestion tagging, `skipped_low_signal: 3`, and a genuine learning still drafting). CHANGELOG entry added under `## Unreleased`.

**Caveats:**
- The three original junk packet files were already hand-deleted from the main repo, so test bodies are plausible reconstructions; each was crafted to pass the *legacy* keyword filters, proving the new gate is what blocks them.
- Self-echo markers are phrase-level (`truth report`, `value_receipt`, ...) — a genuine learning *about* Kage's receipt internals that quotes those exact strings would be gated from auto-drafts (manual `kage learn` still works for that case).
- Manual `kage distill` is intentionally ungated (treated as explicit intent, like learn/capture); only the Stop-hook `--auto` path is filtered.
- Per instructions, `kage refresh` was not run. Also note: no Kage MCP tools were loaded in this session, so the harness calls from CLAUDE.md (kage_context/kage_learn/kage_pr_check) could not be made; a restart would be needed to load them.</result>
